extraction.one_line_summary: HOULE Hugo won from a breakaway in Foix after an all-day escape, outsprinting MADOUAS Valentin in the final 200m after a technical corner sequence.
tactical_patterns: GESCHKE Simon aggressively contested fourth-category points despite already leading KOM, indicating defensive jersey tactics against teammates with gap advantage.
POGAČAR Tadej caught VINGEGAARD Jonas unprepared with first attack on opening Cat-1 climb, forcing reactive bridging at 7-8% gradient speeds.
Technical 180-degree corner with 300m left negated pure sprint power; MADOUAS Valentin led into corner but HOULE held positioning advantage through chicane.

rider_signals.HOULE Hugo: positive: First Tour stage win at age 31, fourth Tour participation, showed strong positioning through technical finale after full day in break
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: positive: Third place from break shows maintained climbing form despite previous stage efforts, fellow Canadian podium presence
rider_signals.JORGENSON Matteo: positive: Fourth place finish from long breakaway indicates emerging American climbing ability in grand tour context
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Generated surprise attacks on Cat-1 climbs at speeds indicating exceptional current form, forced reactive racing from yellow jersey
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: negative: Caught off-guard by first POGAČAR attack, required high-speed bridging effort that suggests momentary tactical lapse
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: negative: Dropped from fourth to ninth overall, significant time loss on mountain stage despite no crash mentioned
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: Moved from sixth to first page of GC through limiting losses while others faltered
rider_signals.GAUDU David: positive: Moved into fifth overall from eighth, capitalized on BARDET collapse
rider_signals.GESCHKE Simon: positive: Actively defending KOM jersey despite teammate POWLESS nine points behind with 23 still available
rider_signals.MADOUAS Valentin: neutral: Led into final corner with strong sprint but positioning through chicane cost stage win
